commit.message:
feat: add venture-agent CLI package (poll, apply, report) (#1957)

* feat: add Venture Factory service task API Edge Functions

Implements three Supabase Edge Functions for the Venture Factory
shared service platform task queue API:
- service-tasks-poll: GET pending tasks with priority ordering
- service-tasks-claim: POST atomic claiming with optimistic locking
- service-tasks-complete: POST completion/failure with confidence scoring

All endpoints enforce venture isolation via RLS policies.

SD: SD-LEO-ORCH-EHG-VENTURE-FACTORY-001-B

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add venture-agent CLI package with poll, apply, report subcommands

Node.js CLI tool (@ehg/venture-agent) for ventures to interact with the
Shared Service Platform. Three subcommands: poll (query pending tasks),
apply (claim/process/complete tasks), report (summary statistics).
Uses commander.js, calls Supabase Edge Functions via fetch with JWT auth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add sticky A-Z index bar to glossary view

- Terms grouped under letter headings (# for non-alpha) with anchor IDs
- Sticky right-side nav shows all 27 keys; active letters are clickable,
  inactive letters are muted — clicking smooth-scrolls to that section
- Works in both All Terms and Standardized views
- Raised glossary fetch limit to 500 to avoid terms being cut off

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix RF coverage overlay not drawing: match ViewshedTool styledata pattern v0.51.1

The styledata handler was calling cleanup() before initLayers(), which
destroyed all sources/layers and re-created them with empty data. This
also triggered infinite styledata loops. Fixed by using the same pattern
as ViewshedTool: only call initLayers on styledata (no cleanup), and
use refs to preserve current geojson data through style changes.

commit.message:
Reduce RPC usage: optimize polling intervals and log scanning

Major changes to cut Alchemy Compute Unit consumption:

- GameService poll: 2s → 10s (biggest offender, ~65K CU/day saved)
- useStakers: scan logs from block 0 ONCE on mount, then only scan
  new blocks; poll interval 15s → 30s (~19K CU/day saved)
- useStakeInfo poll: 5s → 15s (~17K CU/day saved)
- useDrillBalance poll: 5s → 10s (~11K CU/day saved)
- useDrillSupply: read MAX_SUPPLY once (immutable constant), poll
  only totalSupply at 30s instead of both at 15s (~4K CU/day saved)

Estimated savings: ~116K CU/day → ~25K CU/day (~78% reduction)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Major update: 14 new features, dashboard, and CLI subcommands

New modules:
- dashboard.py: interactive startup dashboard with stats overview
- config.py: persistent settings (~/.sectools-config.json)
- hash_id.py: auto-detect hash types (MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, bcrypt, etc.)
- revshell.py: reverse shell generator for 9 languages
- encoding.py: Base64, URL, hex, ROT13, HTML, binary encode/decode
- port_ref.py: 88 common ports lookup with search
- subnet_calc.py: IP/CIDR calculator using stdlib ipaddress
- passgen.py: secure password generator with entropy estimate
- http_probe.py: quick URL scanner (status, headers, SSL, redirects)
- scan_history.py: browse, search, view, and delete past scan logs
- wordlist_mgr.py: list, download, and manage wordlists
- target_groups.py: organize targets into groups, scan groups
- scan_profiles.py: save and reuse custom scan configurations
- auto_installer.py: install missing tools via brew/apt/choco

Updated:
- main.py: reorganized menu with 34 items across 8 categories
- cli.py: new subcommands (version, status, hash, encode)
- notifications.py: respects config notifications_enabled setting
- README.md: comprehensive docs for all features

commit.message:
fix: use position endpoint for race results in OpenF1 loader

Previously, OpenF1 race results were derived from best lap times
which doesn't reflect actual race classification. Now uses the
official position endpoint (like qualifying) to get correct results.

This fixes race results showing incorrect positions (e.g., Verstappen
as winner when Russell actually won in Australia 2026).

as the two typographic voices. Replaces ~550 ad-hoc text-[Xpx] classes and ~57
Tailwind defaults with semantic tokens (text-display-*, text-header-*, text-body-*,
text-caption-*, text-label-*, text-numbers-*) with proper tracking and leading.

feat: add achievement tracker and unlock celebration to second screen (#97)

Phase 3 of the Second Screen Companion:

video: Docker-based render pipeline for Hetzner VPS

- Add Dockerfile using ghcr.io/remotion-dev/base — ships with Chromium
  and all Puppeteer deps pre-baked, no manual apt installs needed
- Add .dockerignore (excludes node_modules, out, .env, media files)
- Update vps-setup.mjs: installs Docker CE instead of Node+Chromium,
  pulls remotion-dev/base image, builds rop-video image on setup
- Update render-vps.mjs: docker build (layer-cached) + docker run

commit.message:
Two-phase glossary standardization: deterministic + LLM with stronger hints

Phase 1 (deterministic, no LLM):
- Normalise all term names (strip punctuation/case)
- Exact-name duplicates ("Adapter" × 2) merged immediately in Python;
  longest definition wins as canonical

Phase 2 (LLM with targeted hints):
- Hint A: Jaccard word-overlap ≥ 0.35 (lowered from 0.5) flags likely pairs
- Hint B: same first content word (≥5 chars) clusters all "agentic *" terms
  together regardless of the words that follow — catches "Agentic Coding" /
  "Agentic AI Coding" / "Agentic Engineering" as one cluster
- Hints labelled "CONFIRMED or VERY LIKELY duplicates — merge them" so LLM
  cannot treat them as merely related

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: CLI subprocess SIGINT isolation + regression tests (#888)

* fix: isolate CLI subprocesses from SIGINT to prevent Ctrl+C respawn

Add start_new_session=True to all CLI subprocess spawns (claude, codex,
gemini). Without this, Ctrl+C sends SIGINT to both Python and the child
process. The child handles SIGINT gracefully and exits with a partial
result, causing auto_finish to spawn a new session instead of stopping.

With start_new_session=True, SIGINT only reaches Python. The asyncio
cancellation then propagates through aclosing() which terminates the
subprocess via the finally block. auto_finish is never reached because
CancelledError propagates out of the async with block.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add CLI cancellation regression tests for SIGINT isolation

12 tests covering:
- start_new_session=True enforcement on all 3 CLI providers
- CancelledError/KeyboardInterrupt skip auto_finish (no respawn)
- _ndjson_from_cli finally block propagates CancelledError
- Tool allowlist/blocklist args have no embedded quotes
- Session default is None (no mutable default sharing)
- Empty responses don't IndexError on auto_finish check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review findings — BaseException guard + test CI reliability

- Add explicit _cancelled sentinel in _call() to prevent auto_finish
  after Task.cancel() (not just SIGINT). Both CancelledError and
  KeyboardInterrupt now skip the second spawn unconditionally.
- Remove pytest.skip guards from mocked tests — the mock makes CLI
  binary availability irrelevant. All 13 tests now run on CI without
  skipping.
- Add test_task_cancel_skips_auto_finish covering the Task.cancel()
  path (non-SIGINT cancellation).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------
